zone_6b_notes: Native wildflower — thrives in Zone 6b Farmington. Extremely tough in Missouri heat, drought, and clay soils. Short-lived perennial that readily self-seeds, so it naturalizes in the garden. Blooms July–September, excellent for pollinators. Leave seed heads standing in fall to feed birds and allow self-seeding.
water: low — drought tolerant; native to Missouri prairies
